stories.title: Hegseth faces questions about Iran in first congressional appearance since war began
stories.description: Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.

stories.title: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.description: Seventeen people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

stories.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
stories.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

stories.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
stories.description: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.description: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.title: In Tehran, money is short and a return to war looms over daily life
stories.description: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Iran, where the buzz of busy shopping streets masks deep uncertainty over the country's future.

While details of the proposal have not yet been released, President Donald Trump said he was "not satisfied" with Iran's latest offer, but negotiations were taking place by...

stories.title: Inside Iran’s ‘mosquito fleet’: Telegraph’s Adrian Blomfield with a rare frontline glimpse of Hormuz
stories.description: Gavin Lee welcomes Adrian Blomfield, Senior Foreign Correspondent at The Telegraph. His reporting from the Strait of Hormuz offers a rare, intimate view beyond satellite imagery and policy abstraction. “One of the things you get a sense of when you're out on the water,” he reflec...

stories.title: Cubans mark May Day amid rising humanitarian crisis
stories.description: People are gathering in cities worldwide to mark International Workers' Day, taking to the streets to demand higher wages and better working conditions. Long observed as a public holiday led by unions advocating for workers’ rights, May 1 has seen large demonstrations this year. ...

stories.title: 60-day deadline for Congress' Iran war extension approval
stories.description: In the United States, lawmakers remain deadlocked over the War Powers Resolution, which requires Congress to approve continued military action or withdraw troops once hostilities reach the 60-day mark. With the deadline approaching and no agreement in sight, a decision remains ou...

stories.title: Do 10-year-old girls really need skincare routines?
stories.description: In France, a new trend is raising eyebrows as an increasing number of children and pre-teens adopt elaborate skincare routines. But behind the glossy promise of perfect skin, experts warn that many of these products are ill-suited to young faces and may even increase the risk of ...

stories.title: 🔴 Middle East war live: Iran has sent its latest proposal to US via Pakistani mediators
Iran war protester speaks to Al Jazeera from top of Washington bridge
stories.description: A 45-year old man is occupying the top of Washington’s Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge to protest the war on Iran.

stories.title: Havana slams new Trump sanctions as ‘collective punishment’ of Cuban people
stories.description: The Cuban government has firmly rejected the recent sanctions levied by US President Donald Trump.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump says no ‘early’ end to war, unhappy with Tehran offer
stories.description: White House tells Congress that Iran hostilities have 'terminated' despite continued US troop presence in Middle East.

stories.title: US said to be withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany over Iran war spat
stories.description: President Donald Trump has feuded with European allies over their reluctance to step up support for war on Iran.

stories.title: Trump considering option to ‘blast the hell out of’ Iran

stories.title: At least 12 killed in latest Israeli attacks on Lebanon
stories.description: Israel's strikes continue, with more than 2,600 killed since March 2, despite an ongoing ceasefire.

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalised as health deteriorates
stories.description: Mohammadi has lost consciousness twice and suffered a severe cardiac crisis, her foundation has announced.

stories.title: Photos: Cuba holds May Day celebrations amid US threats
stories.description: March celebrating International Workers' Day coincided with announcement of further US sanctions on island.

stories.title: US to ‘close its flagship Gaza mission’
stories.description: TEL AVIV: A US military-run body near Gaza that critics say failed in its mission to monitor the Israel-Hamas ceasefire and boost aid flows to besieged Palestinians is set to be shut by the Trump administration, sources familiar with the matter told Reuters. stories.title: Trump's war powers deadline expires Friday as officials cite ceasefire loophole
stories.description: President Donald Trump faces a critical legal deadline on Friday that could determine the future of US military operations against Iran, even as his administration signals it may rely on a controversial legal interpretation to bypass congressional approval. stories.title: UN chief warns Hormuz closure ‘strangling’ global economy
stories.description: UNITED NATIONS: The escalating crisis in the Strait of Hormuz could push tens of millions into poverty, trigger a surge in global hunger and even tip the world toward recession, United Nations Secretary General Antonio Guterres warned on Thursday. stories.title: Berlin strikes defiant tone as Trump-Merz sparring continues

stories.title: Amazon says damaged UAE cloud region recovery to take several months
stories.description: Amazon said on Thursday that restoring cloud computing operations in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), which were damaged in the conflict in the Middle East, is expected to take several months. Amazon’s data centres in the region were hit by Iranian retaliatory drone strikes in ear...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Trump says U.S. Navy acting 'like pirates' to carry out naval blockade

stories.title: Spirit Airlines appears closer to shutdown as time dwindles for government bailout
stories.description: The U.S. President floated the idea of a bailout last week after the airline found itself in bankruptcy proceedings for the second time in less than two years with jet fuel prices soaring because of the Iran war

stories.title: Hezbollah’s fibre-optic drones pose new challenge for Israel
stories.description: Unlike conventional drones guided by GPS or radio, which can therefore be jammed, Hezbollah is using devices linked to their launch site by a thin fibre-optic cable that can stretch for dozens of kilometres

stories.title: Explosion of bombs left over from strikes kills 14 Iranian Revolutionary Guard members, reports say
stories.description: A report by the Nournews website, believed to be close to Iran's security, said the explosion happened near the northern city of Zanjan, which is northwest of Tehran

stories.title: U.S. withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany, U.S. officials say
stories.description: Mr. Trump had threatened a drawdown in forces earlier this week after ​sparring with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, who said the Iranians were humiliating the U.S. in ⁠talks to end the two-month-old war and that he did not see what exit strategy Washington was pursuing
